title: Stelis laevigata
taxonomy: Stelis laevigata is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the genus Stelis. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes approximately 30,000 species of orchids. This classification places Stelis laevigata in the subfamily Epidendroideae, a group of orchids known for their diverse range of species. The subfamily Epidendroideae is further divided into various tribes, including the tribe Epidendreae, to which Stelis laevigata belongs. This detailed classification allows for a more precise understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and taxonomic position.

intro: Stelis laevigata, also known as Stelis, is a plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis laevigata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or ointment to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, often used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
